events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: LEAP After School Program
events.description: This  program offers a fun and engaging environment with two hours of physical activities, trivia, games, crafts, and sports-based activities. Participants will build independence and social skills while enjoying nutritious snacks and educational workshops. The program is safe, supervised, and facilitated in partnership with North York Community House LEAP staff. events.title: Therapy Dog Visits
events.description: Join us for visits with a therapy dog. Children will have an opportunity to read, listen to stories, and engage in therapeutic human-animal interaction. Children who live with a disability, are neurodivergent, or have experienced medical or other types of trauma are especially welcome. This program is presented in partnership with One Health Partners. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Bharatanatyam - Indian Classical Dance Performance
events.description: In honour of Asian Heritage Month, Bendale presents​ a performance of Bharatanatyam, the oldest dance tradition of India. Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: Kimbap Workshop with Chef Sang
events.description: TV host and celebrity chef Sang Kim will teach the basics of making Korean-style kimbap, exactly like you see them on your favourite K-dramas. Participants will roll and cut kimbap using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. A brief history of kimbap, its differences from the Japanese sushi roll, and eating etiquette will also be shared. Prizes are also awarded for best kimbap creations. events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Oat Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classics ingredients including oat milk and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally.

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around Taylor Memorial
events.description: Taylor Memorial Branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Registration is required   The tour will start and end at the Taylor Memorial meeting room and will be guided by the City of Toronto U

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Come and play some fun PS5 or Nintendo Switch 2 games with your friends! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Pomodoro Study Hall
events.description: Join us for a focused study session using the Pomodoro Technique. Perfect for teens looking to boost productivity and stay on track with their schoolwork. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Scratch Art
events.description: Create bookmarks or your own designs as you scratch paper to reveal the art underneath. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: History of Generative Music: The Ancients

You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: Painting the Seasons - A Pipa Solo Concert
events.description: Audience will have this wonderful and rare opportunity to appreciate the unique Chinese music instrument live performance. Internationally renowned Toronto-based Pipa artist Wen Zhao will perform a solo concert celebrating China’s rich musical legacy, with a traditional and folk music repertoire spanning across dynasties. Open to all ages. No registration required, limited to a room capacity.

This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: Accessing the Wisdom of Nature
events.description: The natural world is a source of living wisdom. The stream has something to say. So does the wind in the branches, the moss on a wall, the path a crow takes across the sky. We will talk about traditions across different cultures that can help us access this wisdom and spend time outdoors tuning into it. Then we will bring those messages back inside to reflect, illustrate, and share. The walk will start and end at the Malvern Branch. There may be uneven, unpaved surfaces along the route. Ple

events.title: Create Hip-Hop Music with AI Tools for Teens
events.description: Have you ever had a melody stuck in your head and wished you could turn it into a real song? AI tools are making it easier than ever to bring your musical experiences to life - but how do you make sure the music you create is truly yours? Explore how to use AI as a creative assistant to build radio-ready hip-hop tracks based on your own original ideas. events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK

events.title: History of Generative Music: The Last 50 Years
events.description: From the first computer-generated compositions to the AI music tools available today, the past five decades have seen generative music evolve at an extraordinary pace. What began as experiments in university labs has become technology that anyone can access from a laptop or phone. events.title: Henna Art with Hina
events.description: Celebrate Asian Heritage Month and discover the art of henna, decorative designs which are created on a person's body, using a paste of natural organic henna. Observe and enjoy the henna application and learn about its history, recipe and traditions. Led by Henna Artist, Hina Yasir. No registration required, all ages, first come, first serve! Come by early to secure your spot, taking place on the main floor of the library.

events.title: Sir Henry Pallatt and the English Trip of 1910

events.title: Steelpan: Hands On Fun!
events.description: Musician Suzette Vidale leads participants in a hands-on experience with the steelpan! ​Have fun while learning about the instrument, the proper technique of holding sticks, stance and playing a scale. Suzette will teach everyone a simple song for participants to play. No musical knowledge required! This session is for adult participants. Mature teens are welcome. Younger participants can join the 3:30pm session. Please register for a spot. Information Collection Notice: The personal inform

events.title: Saturday Knit & Crochet Circle
events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno.  Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: How to Read Poetry: Seeing, Hearing, Feeling
events.description: Coach Desiree McKenzie teaches participants how to analyze rhythm, tone and imagery in poetry and apply these analytical skills and techniques into their own writing.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: All skill levels are welcome! Chess sets are provided.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Bars & Beats
events.description: Coach Desiree McKenzie guides participants through an exploration of rap music as poetry in motion. Participants will analyze lyrics and create their own original spoken word pieces inspired by musical flow and technique.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Teen Quiet Study
events.description: Are you looking for a quiet space to study after school? Join our quiet study hall at Locke's program room every Thursday for individual or group study. Tables and chairs are provided, as well as a variety of materials to take study breaks. This is a drop-in program for Teens, and space is limited.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. March 19: Michael Clayton (2007, 14A)  March 26: Ocean’s Twelve (2004, PG) April 2: Bridesmaids (2011, 14A) April 9: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery (1997, PG-13) April 16: Meet the Parents (2000, PG) April 23: Rounders (1998, R) April 30: No Country for Old Men (2007, 18A) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration r

events.title: Silent Book Club
events.description: Come bring a book you are reading. We read for 30-45 minutes and then everyone talks about what they have read. We meet the second Thursday of every month from 7:00 to 8:00 pm.

events.description: Play Dungeons and Dragons! Slay monsters and overcome challenges with a Librarian as your Dungeon Master. Ongoing Campaign. This is an in-person program for Teens. Space limited for players*, but watchers are welcome too. All supplies provided. Bring a 5th level character or pick a Pre-generated character. *Register in-person at the Youth Hub at 3:30PM. events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Join us at the Bloor/Gladstone Library to play chess with friends, family, and fellow enthusiasts! Players of all ages and strengths are welcome, no previous experience is required. Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Sat. Feb. 28.

events.title: Create Pop Music with AI Tools for Teens
events.description: Have you ever had a melody stuck in your head and wished you could turn it into a real song? AI tools are making it easier than ever to bring your musical experiences to life - but how do you make sure the music you create is truly yours? Explore how to use AI as a creative assistant to build radio-ready pop tracks based on your own original ideas. You'll learn how to guide AI tools to serve your vision rather than replace it, and we'll cover Canadian copyright guidelines to ensure the music you

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver.
